Off Calendar Jan-16-2018 Continued

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Oct-07-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for and with (executor) Thomas Lucas.

Mr. Siracusa addressed the examiners notes regarding the credit card and that the care facility payment was made with Mr. Lucas' credit card, that it bounced and he was not aware of it, that the facility sent it to collections without letting Mr. Lucas know, that it took time to get the receipt and then was immediately paid, that they do not believe that there was a loss to the estate or if so maybe a few dollars of interest. Mr. Siracusa addressed the issue regarding the creditors claim stating that they addressed it in the petition, that they realize it needed to be approved by the court, that if the court does not accept this it can reduce the fees and requested that it be a voluntary reduction as opposed to a surcharge so that Mr. Lucas' bonding will not be affected. Mr. Siracusa addressed the bond issue stating that they are well aware of the need to check the bond amount, that somehow they missed this one and that they immediately filed an ex parte petition to increase the amount. Mr. Siracusa addressed the failure to spread out money in accounts stating that they have to be combined to do distribution, that it would have only been spread out for a couple of weeks and that it is all set to issue checks. Mr. Siracusa further stated that this was a time consuming case, that there was a huge number of treasury bonds, that there were various beneficiaries and that they sold the property. The Court stated it believes the gravity is realized and that it has concerns regarding anything that seems self dealing. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Watts. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Sep-14-2015 Continued To Oct-05-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (2087 20th Avenue, San Francisco, Ca 94116)
Off Calendar Mar-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Thomas A. Lucas.

There being no overbids, the sale was confirmed at $775,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $744,000.00 is required.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Allen. (Not Reported)
